hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite papers with future publication years (2025, 2026) or arXiv IDs that do not correspond to real publications. Examples include Singhal et al. (2025), Xu et al. (2025), Samu et al. (2026), Tao et al. (2025a/b), and others. These appear fabricated to support claims about human-AI collaboration and uncertainty communication.
hallucinations.evidence: References section lists: 'Karan Singhal et al. (2025)', 'Gerui Xu et al. (2025)', 'Most. Sharmin Sultana Samu et al. (2026)', 'Linwei Tao et al. (2025a/b)', and similar entries with implausible future dates and non-existent arXiv identifiers.

overall_assessment: The paper contains hallucinations primarily in the form of nonexistent citations with fabricated future-year references. The core methodology, synthetic data generation, model architecture, loss functions, and metrics (ARR, TCE, TCS) align with the provided code. Experimental claims about early peaks and degradation are plausible given the code's early-stopping and ablation results, though some specific numeric outcomes may be idealized. No clear evidence of faked results or mathematical errors, but citation issues undermine credibility.
